hard.next_step: 6/18/26 Perdue assessing vendor responses, will reconnect in ~30 days (mid-July). RFI response submitted 6/1, scripted demo delivered 6/2 by Devica. 6/1 Perdue clarified direct-spend requirements were for other vendors; primary focus is best-of-breed indirect S2P.
hard.ais_why: Formal RFI for a Source-to-Pay platform with conventional, functionality-driven requirements (strategic sourcing, RFx, bid analysis, procure-to-pay, ERP integration); no AI priority stated. Fit concerns: Zycus nearly declined over direct-spend/LIMS/QMS requirements and limited MS Teams usage (Zoom chat), which undercuts Merlin conversational intake. The 6/2 session was a ~90-min product demo; all follow-ups are functional/integration-driven (two- vs three-way match, SAP adapters/GRIR, manual invoice validation, down-select use cases), no AI raised as a priority. Selection is S2P functional-fit-led, not AI-led. Formal Evaluation, 20%.
hard.competitor: Agiloft CLM (incumbent, iContract out of scope); IBM Maximo (incumbent P2P being displaced); undisclosed S2P vendors in the multi-vendor RFI; status-quo/do-nothing

north_star_verdict.headline: Active, functional-fit-led indirect S2P RFI. Zycus delivered a strong scripted demo 2 Jun to the full evaluation team and is back in scope after clarifying direct spend was for other vendors; buyer is assessing and reconnects mid-July. Win depends on proving SAP/Maximo/Agiloft integration and finding a real advocate beyond coordinator Neil Schloss against an unnamed competitive field.
north_star_verdict.math: 199 days to close (21 Jan 2027), 47 days into the cycle (created 20 May), all time in Formal Evaluation (no submission-date fields populated for granular time-in-stage). No forward slip in field history. Required pace: down-select outcome Aug/Sep, commercials + EB proposal Oct/Nov, contracting/InfoSec Nov/Dec to hold the Jan close. On pace given buyer commitment (18 Jun) to reconnect ~mid-July after assessing responses. Last verified SF activity 25 Jun (11 days ago); rep outreach 18 Jun awaiting the buyer's mid-July reconnect.
evidence: RFI received and MNDA executed 20 May; RFI response submitted 1 Jun; scripted use-case demo delivered 2 Jun by Devica to 8+ evaluators (Next Step 1 Jun; Avoma demo 2 Jun).
Zycus was back-in-scope after Perdue clarified 1 Jun that direct-spend/LIMS/QMS requirements were included for other vendors and the primary focus is best-of-breed indirect S2P (Next Step 1 Jun).

Buyer committed 18 Jun to assess vendor responses and reconnect in ~30 days, i.e. mid-July (Next Step 18 Jun; Neil Schloss email 15 Jun 'not for a month or so until we have digested everything we saw with the Demos').
Perdue issued an internal scoring survey to the evaluation team 4 Jun ('please review and submit your scoring survey'), confirming a structured, active evaluation (Task 4 Jun).

Deep buyer engagement on the 2 Jun demo from the center-of-excellence lead (Ami) on agentic sourcing (Anna), touchless POs, OCR accuracy and AP automation, but no buyer preference stated and the evaluation is functional-fit-led (Avoma demo 2 Jun).

Pipeline is defensible for an early competitive Formal-Evaluation RFI with no economic-buyer commercial conversation; it should not be promoted to Best Case/Commit until the mid-July down-select outcome and EB engagement are confirmed.

metrics.narrative: Perdue articulated clear qualitative pain but no quantified value case. On the 2 Jun demo Neil Schloss said they use Maximo for P2P and 'what we're looking for is basically a best in class p to p or s to p solution set because that's not maximo strength', and the buyer team confirmed fragmentation across roughly seven systems, manual PO flips ('most of our POs are flipped today'), and email-based invoice processing with no OCR ('we're piloting one'). Zycus asserted its own metrics (70% faster intake, 7-8 month implementation, 99.5% uptime) but Perdue named no cost, headcount, cycle-time, error-rate or savings target. Quantifying the business case with the procurement and AP owners is the ranked move so Zycus can anchor commercials and an ROI case for the economic buyer.
sources: Avoma demo 2 Jun (Neil Schloss on Maximo P2P gap; buyer team on manual PO flips and email invoices)
SF AIS_Why (functional, no AI/ROI priority)
SF Products (full indirect S2P suite implies transformation scope)

economic_buyer.narrative: Richard Morin, Vice President Corporate Controller, is the inferred economic buyer: as corporate controller he owns the finance/AP domain the deal most affects, and he is a named OpportunityContactRole contact who was on the 2 Jun follow-up distribution ('Neil, Ami, Sal, Christina, Gail, Ashley, Kevin, Marcelo, Richard and team'). No commercial, pricing, or budget conversation has occurred with him and there is no evidence he is driving the evaluation, so his engagement is evaluative, not sponsorship. The business owner running the procurement side appears to be the procurement division head heard on the call (Sal Gala), but he is not a Salesforce-verified contact so his authority is unconfirmed. Confirming who owns the budget and the approval chain above Richard Morin is a ranked move.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Richard Morin, VP Corporate Controller)
Avoma demo 2 Jun (Richard on follow-up distribution; procurement-division head present)
SF Task 2 Jun (follow-up recap email addressed to Richard and team)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are functional-fit-led and visible from the RFI scenarios and the 2 Jun demo. Perdue supplied detailed scenarios in advance ('we did get the scenarios that you gave us... it was pretty detailed') covering intake, sourcing, contract-to-PO flip, requisitions, touchless POs, service/time sheets, invoicing and two- vs three-way match. Buyer questions revealed weighted criteria: integration depth with SAP, Maximo, Agiloft CLM, Vertex tax and their HR system; touchless-PO thresholds and compliance rules; OCR/AI accuracy (Zycus stated ~90%); supplier-portal adoption; and change management given Perdue is Zoom-first. The formal scorecard weighting is not yet shared (an internal scoring survey went out 4 Jun), so criteria are confirmed in substance but the ranking of what wins is still inferred.
sources: Avoma demo 2 Jun (buyer scenarios and Ami/Ashley/finance questions on integration, touchless POs, OCR, tax validation)
SF Task 4 Jun (internal scoring survey issued)
SF AIS_Why (functional S2P selection, not AI-led)

decision_process.narrative: Perdue is running a structured multi-vendor RFI with a broad evaluation committee. The sequence is clear from the trail: RFI received and MNDA executed 20 May, clarification questions 22 May, RFI response submitted 1 Jun, scripted demo 2 Jun, internal scoring survey issued 4 Jun, then a self-directed assessment window with a commitment to reconnect in ~30 days (mid-July) before a down-select. The 2 Jun call showed the evaluating body: procurement division leadership, a center-of-excellence lead, multiple category managers (Christina Sandefur, Ashley Towne), IT/technical (Gail Wallace) and finance (Richard Morin on the recap). The approval chain above the committee and the number of vendors in the RFI are not yet mapped, so the process is well understood at the evaluation layer but opaque at the decision/approval layer.
sources: SF Next Step 20 May, 27 May, 1 Jun, 18 Jun (RFI, MNDA, response, reconnect timing)
SF Task 4 Jun (scoring survey)
Avoma demo 2 Jun (evaluation-committee composition)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in play, which is normal at Formal Evaluation. The only paper executed is the mutual NDA (MNDA facilitated 20 May). No MSA, Order Form, SOW, pricing, legal review, procurement-onboarding or InfoSec track has opened. Perdue just renewed its Agiloft CLM ('just get a new renewal with them'), so iContract is out of scope and Zycus should not weight a CLM contracting path. Visibility will come from the mid-July reconnect: securing the down-select outcome and then asking Perdue to walk through its procurement/legal path (MSA vs their paper, InfoSec/security questionnaire owner, and who signs) is what converts this from unknown to mapped.
sources: SF Next Step 20 May (MNDA executed)
Avoma demo 2 Jun (Agiloft CLM just renewed, iContract out of scope)

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is clearly articulated by the buyer, though unquantified. Neil Schloss described disparate systems after parting ways with Zycus iContract/iProcurement three years ago and using Maximo for P2P, which 'is not maximo strength'. The team confirmed no intake process today ('we don't have it today, Devika'), mostly manual PO flips, email-driven invoices with no OCR, and receiving delays at plants/manufacturing sites ('sometimes at our plants... it's delayed'). Zycus framed the pain as fragmentation across seven systems, limited visibility, manual processing and compliance risk. The pain owners are the procurement division (sourcing/requisitioning) and AP/finance under Richard Morin (invoice matching, payment queries). Pain is confirmed; converting it to a quantified cost is the outstanding step.
sources: Avoma demo 2 Jun (Neil on Maximo P2P gap and disparate systems; team on no intake, manual PO flips, invoice/receiving delays)
SF AIS_Why (manual invoice validation)

champion.narrative: Neil Schloss is the identified champion but his advocacy is developing, not strong. He owns procurement technology as a category, is the single point of contact across every Next Step (20 May, 27 May, 1 Jun, 18 Jun), booked the demo slot, and was transparent and helpful, notably clarifying on 1 Jun that the direct-spend requirements were for other vendors, which brought Zycus back into scope after Zycus nearly declined on 27 May. What is missing is genuine championship: no evidence he is selling Zycus internally, influencing the scorecard, blocking rivals, sharing competitive standing, or introducing Zycus to Richard Morin or the procurement division head. He reads as a process coordinator. He also has no Salesforce OpportunityContactRole (a hygiene gap). Assessing his real influence and cultivating a second advocate with a scorecard vote is the ranked move.
sources: SF Next Step 20 May, 27 May, 1 Jun, 18 Jun (Neil primary contact and scope-clarifier)
SF Task contacts (Neil Schloss)
Avoma demo 2 Jun (Neil facilitated, no advocacy shown)

competition.narrative: The competitive picture is partly known and critically incomplete. Agiloft is the confirmed incumbent CLM (replaced Zycus iContract three years ago and just renewed on the 2 Jun call), so iContract is out of scope and Agiloft is a defended boundary, not an S2P rival to beat. IBM Maximo is the incumbent P2P being displaced, which is favorable to Zycus since the buyer explicitly wants best-in-class S2P beyond Maximo. The real threat is twofold: the undisclosed set of other S2P vendors in this multi-vendor RFI (Perdue is 'assessing vendor responses'), whose names Zycus does not have, and the status-quo/do-nothing risk of Perdue continuing to run procurement across seven manual systems given no compelling event. Zycus's wedge is the organically-built single-platform story that resonated with Neil's disparate-systems pain, plus integration to protect the Agiloft investment rather than rip-and-replace. Uncovering the RFI field is a ranked move; ranking rivals blind is not possible.
sources: Avoma demo 2 Jun (Agiloft renewed, iContract out of scope; Maximo P2P being replaced; single-platform pitch)
SF Next Step 1 Jun, 18 Jun (multi-vendor RFI, vendor assessment in progress)

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is real and rep-forward since the 2 Jun demo. The deal recovered from a near self-disqualification on 27 May, expanded in value, and is now in the buyer's self-directed assessment window ahead of a mid-July reconnect.

items.change: RFI received and mutual NDA executed; sent to solution consulting for review
Zycus nearly declined on functional fit (direct spend, LIMS/QMS integration, limited MS Teams), then stage moved Qualified Out to Formal Evaluation after buyer clarification
Perdue clarified indirect S2P is the primary focus (direct-spend requirements were for other vendors); amount raised from $400k to $613k; RFI response submitted
Scripted use-case demo delivered by Devica to the full evaluation committee
Perdue issued an internal scoring survey to evaluation-team attendees
Neil Schloss confirmed Perdue will assess responses and reconnect in ~30 days (mid-July)

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest CURRENT threat is the undisclosed set of other S2P vendors in this multi-vendor RFI (Perdue is assessing responses as of 18 Jun) plus the status-quo risk of Perdue staying on manual, Maximo-based processes with no compelling event. Agiloft is the incumbent CLM (just renewed 2 Jun) but sits OUTSIDE S2P scope so it is a low current threat; IBM Maximo is the incumbent P2P being displaced, which favors Zycus. Zycus is competing blind on the rival field, which is the top competitive gap to close before the down-select.

competitors.quote: We've been using Maximo for the p two p functionality... and what we're looking for is basically a best in class p to p or s to p solution set because that's not maximo strength.
competitors.how_we_win: Create a compelling event by quantifying the cost of manual PO flips and email-invoice processing so inertia becomes the expensive option.

competitors.how_we_win: Position best-in-class S2P while integrating back to Maximo for asset/maintenance so Perdue keeps Maximo where it is strong.

competitors.how_we_win: Protect the Agiloft investment with a clean sourcing-to-Agiloft and contract-consume-back integration rather than displacing CLM.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Perdue wants best-in-class indirect S2P on one platform to replace fragmented manual processes; Zycus fits the core well but is exposed on integration depth, the direct-spend/LIMS/QMS boundary, and Zoom-first change management.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most now: multi-thread beyond a coordinator-style champion, prove the integration story that is the buyer's stated risk, and lock the indirect-only scope that nearly disqualified Zycus.
flags: Multi-thread past Neil: engage the procurement division head and Richard Morin (finance) directly before the mid-July down-select, since Neil reads as a coordinator, not a proven advocate.
Close the integration-proof gap: SAP, Maximo, Agiloft CLM and Vertex integration is Perdue's stated risk ('the data that connects... we don't have') and will decide the functional evaluation.
Get written confirmation that indirect S2P (not direct-spend/LIMS/QMS) is the scoring scope, so the 27 May fit objection cannot resurface and sink the deal.

items.name: Richard Morin
items.title: Vice President Corporate Controller
items.sentiment: Engaged evaluatively (on the 2 Jun demo recap distribution; finance owner of the AP/invoice domain), not yet in any commercial conversation.
items.risk: No pricing or budget discussion yet; if he is not the final approver, the chain above him is unmapped.
items.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; Avoma demo 2 Jun recap distribution

items.name: Neil Schloss
items.sentiment: Responsive, transparent, facilitating the RFI and scope clarification; advocacy still developing (coordinator, not internal seller).
items.risk: May lack scorecard influence in the down-select; Zycus could be left without a true internal advocate versus a rival with a stronger one.
items.source: SF Task contacts; Next Step 20 May-18 Jun; Avoma demo 2 Jun

items.name: Mark Passen
items.title: Regional Purchasing Manager
items.sentiment: Regional purchasing stakeholder evaluating eProcurement/regional workflow fit; no direct engagement evidence yet.

champion_strength.summary: Neil Schloss is the champion but at a developing level. Confirmed advocacy signals: single point of contact across every Next Step, booked the demo, and clarified on 1 Jun that direct-spend requirements were for other vendors, which brought Zycus back into scope. Still missing: internal selling, scorecard influence, competitor blocking, and an introduction to Richard Morin or the procurement division head. He is a process coordinator until proven otherwise.
champion_strength.source: SF Next Step 20 May, 27 May, 1 Jun, 18 Jun; SF Task contacts; Avoma demo 2 Jun

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led hard with AI on the 2 Jun demo (Merlin intake orchestration, Anna autonomous negotiation, AP Smartdesk OCR) and drew genuine buyer curiosity from the center-of-excellence lead, but Perdue frames its needs functionally (touchless automation, integration), not AI-first, and the AIS reads Negative. The agentic story is being shown but is not yet the buyer's stated differentiator.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 3/Negative: a functional S2P RFI with no AI priority stated and fit concerns (direct spend, Zoom-not-Teams undercutting Merlin conversational intake). Calls add nuance: the center-of-excellence lead engaged deeply on Anna agentic sourcing, touchless POs and 90% OCR accuracy, so appetite for automation exists even though AI is not the stated selection driver.

items.detail: Heavy dependency on SAP ERP, Maximo, Agiloft CLM, Vertex tax and HR integration; buyer flagged contract-to-PO line-item data they 'don't have today'.
Perdue is Zoom-first while Merlin conversational intake sits in Teams/Slack (web fallback only), undercutting the AI-intake story.
Direct-spend/LIMS/QMS requirements nearly disqualified Zycus on 27 May; clarified out of scope but could resurface in scoring.
Multi-vendor RFI with the competitive field unnamed and no read on scorecard standing; Zycus is positioning blind.
No economic-buyer commercial conversation and no quantified ROI; the value case for a $613k platform is unbuilt.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live but in a buyer-controlled assessment window; all touches since the 2 Jun demo are rep-initiated follow-up awaiting the mid-July reconnect.
items: RFI response (1 Jun), scripted demo (2 Jun) and internal scoring survey (4 Jun) show a committed, structured evaluation.
No two-way buyer engagement since 2 Jun; the buyer is assessing responses and reconnects ~mid-July (rep outreach 18 Jun awaiting reply).
Deal recovered from a near self-disqualification (27 May) and expanded in value ($400k to $613k, 1 Jun).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Perdue is a live, functional-fit-led indirect S2P RFI where Zycus delivered a strong scripted demo on 2 Jun and is now awaiting the mid-July down-select; the win hinges on proving SAP/Maximo/Agiloft integration and finding a real advocate beyond process-coordinator Neil Schloss, against an unnamed competitive field.

win_position.text: Zycus is a live participant in Perdue's indirect S2P RFI after clarifying on 1 Jun that direct-spend/LIMS/QMS requirements were for other vendors, and delivered a full scripted demo on 2 Jun to the entire evaluation committee.

The evaluation is functional-fit-led with a Negative AIS (3), and Zycus nearly self-disqualified on 27 May over direct-spend/LIMS/QMS gaps, so any resurfacing of those requirements in scoring is a live loss risk.
No buyer preference has been expressed and the rest of the RFI field is unnamed, so Zycus is competing blind with only a developing, coordinator-style champion in Neil Schloss.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is real and recent: RFI response submitted 1 Jun, scripted demo delivered 2 Jun, scoring survey issued to the team 4 Jun, and Neil committed 18 Jun to reconnect in ~30 days (mid-July).
Every touch since the 2 Jun demo has been rep-initiated follow-up; the buyer has gone into a self-directed assessment window with no new two-way engagement.

customer_commitment.text: Perdue invested a 90-minute scripted demo with 8+ evaluators including the procurement division head, a center-of-excellence lead, IT and finance, executed a mutual NDA on 20 May, and issued an internal scoring survey on 4 Jun.
Commitment is procedural, not commercial: no pricing, ROI, or economic-buyer conversation has occurred and no firm decision date exists beyond 'reconnect in ~30 days'.

deal_risk.text: Heavy integration dependency (SAP, Maximo P2P, Agiloft CLM, Vertex, HR) with buyer-flagged data prerequisites they 'don't have today' is the top functional risk.

Change-management friction (Perdue is Zoom-first while Merlin intake sits in Teams/Slack) undercuts the AI-intake story that the AIS already rates Negative, and the economic buyer is only inferred and unengaged on commercials.

integration_risk.evidence: Buyer flagged missing contract-to-PO line-item data and multi-system integration (SAP/Maximo/Agiloft/Vertex) on the 2 Jun demo.

commercial_engagement.evidence: No pricing/ROI or economic-buyer commercial conversation has occurred; forecast is Pipeline.

customer_preference.evidence: Deep functional engagement on the 2 Jun demo but no Zycus preference stated; buyer is 'assessing vendor responses' in a multi-vendor RFI with a Negative AIS and a near-disqualification on fit.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (Maximo P2P gap, no intake, manual PO flips, email-invoice processing, receiving delays); no cost, cycle-time, headcount or savings figure quantified.

scope_change.to: $613k full indirect S2P suite (iContract/CLM carved out after Agiloft renewal)
scope_change.detail: Amount rose from $400k to $613k on 1 Jun as scope firmed to best-of-breed indirect S2P; iContract removed since Perdue just renewed Agiloft CLM.

items.action: Confirm the mid-July reconnect with Neil and request RFI/demo feedback plus criteria weighting.
items.trigger: Neil committed to assess responses and reconnect in ~30 days after the 2 Jun demo.
items.expected_effect: Locks the next buyer touch and surfaces where Zycus stands before the down-select.

items.action: Ask Neil who owns the budget and holds the down-select vote, then request access to that leader.
items.trigger: Economic buyer only inferred (Richard Morin); procurement division head is transcript-only and unengaged.
items.expected_effect: Maps real decision authority and multi-threads beyond a coordinator champion.

items.action: Ask Neil which other vendors are in the RFI to activate competitive positioning.
items.trigger: Multi-vendor RFI with the competitive field unnamed as of 18 Jun.
items.expected_effect: Removes the competitive blind spot so the single-platform and AI-automation wedge can be aimed.

items.action: Send SAP-certified adapter, GRIR and two- vs three-way match integration proof, and propose a use-case down-select session.
items.trigger: Buyer flagged missing contract-to-PO data and integration dependencies on the 2 Jun demo.
items.expected_effect: Neutralizes the top functional risk that decides the scorecard.

items.action: Build an ROI model on manual PO flips, email-invoice processing and intake to give the economic buyer a value case.
items.trigger: Pain is confirmed but unquantified; no commercial conversation with the economic buyer yet.
items.expected_effect: Creates a compelling event and anchors the $613k commercials for EB sign-off.

critical_signals.text: On the 2 Jun scripted demo Zycus showed the full S2P suite plus Anna agentic sourcing and AP OCR to 8+ evaluators, drawing deep questions from the center-of-excellence lead, but the buyer stated no preference and is now scoring responses internally.

This is a multi-vendor RFI with the other S2P vendors unnamed; Agiloft is the just-renewed incumbent CLM (out of scope) and Maximo is the P2P being displaced, so the real fight is an unnamed field plus the status quo.
Perdue surfaced integration data prerequisites they do not have today (contract-to-PO line-item data) and confirmed they are Zoom-first, which limits Zycus's Teams-based conversational intake.
The procurement division head and a center-of-excellence lead appeared as senior evaluators on the 2 Jun demo but Zycus has not engaged them directly outside the group session.
The deal grew to $613k on 1 Jun yet no pricing, ROI or economic-buyer commercial conversation has happened, leaving the value case for the platform unbuilt.
